text:
Conclusions (I)
M. Sc. Thesis: Open- and closed-loop Doppler data modelling for spacecraft tracking M. Mezo Gómez
27
Is it possible to convert open-loop Doppler data into equivalent closed-
loop Doppler observations by means of a mathematical model?
• Yes, it is.
• Two different approaches have been examined along this study.
• Both have shown to be capable of achieving conversion accuracies of the order of the accuracy targets
under certain conditions.

B) XDEQ Inventory losses claims

S: A commercial negotiation was blocked over a €6Mn inventory loss claim against a 3PL, where 50% of the disputed amount was linked to what I believed were unfair claims due to a systems connection gap, which resulted in Amz overpaying inventory suppliers. 

T: Use my BI expertise to unlock the negotiation, protect the commercial relationship (€20 Mn+/yr), and ensure a data-driven, fair claim.

3. Multis enablement at NS 3PL

S: Identified a critical capability gap at 3PL sites—inability to ship multi-unit orders in one package—leading to poor CX and PR exposure. I proactively took ownership of the project, which was an unprecedented initiative. 

T: Estimate the full financial cost/benefit and successfully implement multi-unit shipping capability at existing 3PL sites without operational disruption.

A: Built a comprehensive financial model ($10M USD savings). Led the complex, cross-functional implementation project (system configuration, process design, training, quality monitoring). Creatively proposed and executed a pilot at zero capex (by leveraging loaned equipment/refurbished rebin setup) eliminating procurement lead time. Developed documentation for a scalable and fast rollout strategy.

R: Successfully enabled multi-unit shipping across 12 EU sites, saving $10M USD in transport costs and 1.5MM packages in shipping supplies, while maintaining quality standards.



Customer Obsession: Relentless focus on fixing CX issues. Craftsmanship: Creative, detailed system design and execution (zero-capex). Technical Expertise: Combining product strategy (capability gap) with technical execution.

7. Technical BI & Data management structure for the whole EF

7. Technical BI & Data management structure for the whole EF

S: The External Fulfillment organization was growing rapidly (~400 headcount, ~185 nodes) and relied on multiple scattered data resources for operational reviews, but the existing infrastructure was prone to errors, hard to maintain, and lacked scalability. 

T: Overhaul the entire data and BI infrastructure (single metrics pipeline) to simplify provisioning, reduce maintenance, ensure scalability, and establish a single SOT

A: Proactively migrated the main data sharing tool from SharePoint to a centralized QuickSight dashboard platform to ensure data consistency with the WBR 'source of truth.' Automated data pipelines to mark new nodes as active by default. Integrated new metrics (SSP, DEA) and aligned late slam metric implementation to company-wide standards. Automated MBR/QBR data preparation, reducing monthly effort by ~6 hours.

R: Established a robust, scalable data structure rooted in a single source of truth, enabling enhanced visibility that drove outstanding operational improvements in DEA-EF (300 to 90bps), Scan compliance (75 to +95%), and Concessions (12k to 9k dpmo).

Craftsmanship: Getting system architecture details right (simplification, scalability, consistency). Trust: Ensuring data consistency and a single 'source of truth.' Technical Expertise: System design and data management structure.

13. Create from scratch a long term truckload forecast

S: Transportation Procurement requested an urgent, rolling 52-week loads forecast with high granularity (week-flow-domicile) to support a high-stakes, time-sensitive $80Mn CAPEX decision for trailer fleet planning. The deadline was 5 weeks after rejection in tech intake. 

T: Develop and deliver a reliable long-term forecast on time that the customer would trust over existing complex ML models or high-level financial plan estimates.

A: Led the effort to create a detailed, manual model (50k entries, 3.5Mn truckloads) by individually parametrizing and modeling complex business flows. Crucially, I successfully aligned the customer to rely on this manual model over a competing ML solution due to its inherent ability to bridge and explain any inaccuracy (avoiding the "blackbox" problem). Prototyped, backtested, eval comparison vs ML timeseries.

R: Delivered the forecast on time for the CAPEX decision, demonstrating high accuracy (e.g. 5% dev in peak weeks). This project delivered $13Mn/yr in savings (reduced attainment loss/premiums) and supported the $80Mn CAPEX investment (2k x $40k/trailer).



Trust: Building confidence in an explainable model over a complex one. Intensity: Playing to win on a high-stakes, time-sensitive project. Problem-Solving/Analytical: Modeling complex business flows and justifying the solution.

2. XDEQ Pickshorts spike recovery

S: A newly launched 3PL site (new provider) experienced a severe quality incident (PS spike to 120k dpmo vs 1.5k target), creating a crisis situation (underperforming site, management turnover). I was sent onsite to urgently restore quality KPI to protect Customer Experience (CX). 

T: Take the lead in a crisis, mobilize resources, restore quality to best-ever levels (for the site), and establish sustainable quality standards for future sites.

A: Acted as an authority and partner to mobilize their team (e.g., sharing VP-level visibility and site benchmark). Ordered an immediate full operational halt (first ever) to conduct a Wall-to-Wall (W2W) count and reported the disaster level internally. Redesigned the PS operational process (physical separators in ground floor, adding permission controls blocking skipping of hard picks, monitoring associate behavior compliance and enforcing i. alternative pick exhaustion and ii. clean system
compliance and enforcing i. alternative pick exhaustion and ii. clean system records of missing inventory in failed/alternative pick). Set up a progress tracking mechanism for leadership.

R: Quality was restored to pre-spike (8k) levels in 4 weeks, and then to a best-ever level (3k DPMO) in 8 weeks. The new, robust PS process and standards were adopted by 3 other new fulfillment nodes launched by the same provider.



Intensity: Dropping everything to fix a critical issue; high drive for excellence. Leadership: Mobilizing resources and driving execution in a crisis. Craftsmanship: Redesigning and standardizing operational processes.

10. Bug in IXD scheduling refresh logic...

10. Bug in IXD scheduling refresh logic...

S: Continuous truck shortage escalations and OB yard congestion were occurring due to faulty IXD truck scheduling, costing the company in urgent, premium-rate mini-tours. 

T: Identify the hidden root cause of the scheduling errors and implement a fix to stop the chronic shortage.

A: I performed a deep dive into scheduling inputs and execution, discovering that the scheduling refresh logic was critically flawed: it failed to account for scheduled loads already consumed/loaded in the previous week. I then implemented a manual stop-gap process and initiated the long-term tech intake for a system fix.

R: The chronic rolling shortage was stopped by scheduling an extra 150-200 trucks/week in a newly dedicated process, saving an estimated > $1 Mn/yr in adhoc/minitour cost premiums



Craftsmanship: Getting the details right by finding and fixing a core system logic flaw. Problem-Solving: Identifying a hidden, high-impact technical root cause.

5. MIGA

5. MIGA

S: IRDR data reported by 3PLs was suspected to be fake (too low, inconsistent with other metrics like PS), undermining quality KPI reliability and defect reduction efforts. 

T: Develop and implement a compliance program to ensure 3PLs follow the required IRDR count methodology, thereby improving data trustworthiness.

A: I conducted onsite process audits to prove deviations, then collaborated to elaborate an SOP for 3PL Managers to conduct their own regular audits. I also incorporated new contractual requirements into the 3PL Ops Manual and aligned regional leaders to mandate the use of the SOP twice a year across 25 different 3PLs.

R: Reported defects increased by 50% (2.8k DPMO to 4.2k DPMO), successfully validating the compliance program's effectiveness and leading to a standardized, contractually-backed process across 25 different 3PLs in the EU.



Craftsmanship: Establishing standardization and clear process specifications. Trust: Making data more trustworthy and securing contractual compliance.

11. Reducing truck fill rate planning inaccuracy...

11. Reducing truck fill rate planning inaccuracy...

S: WHT planning suffered from poor Truck Fill Rate (TFR) accuracy (35% WAPE) and high operational disruption (30% add/cxl rate), leading to inflated transport costs. 

T: Improve TFR planning accuracy through data-driven experimentation and reduce operational disruption.

A: I initiated iterative experimentation with various forecasting assumptions (segregate PL/LL, sensitivity to weeks qty for avg on historicals, etc). I then recognized that the manual site-provided configuration plan did not add incremental value, enabling me to fully automate the TFR planning process.

R: TFR WAPE was reduced from 35% to 17%. This improved planning reduced the add/cxl rate by 500 bps, yielding estimated annual savings of > $1.8Mn (saved adhoc premiums of 10% of 7k*0.05=350 trucks saved/wk), all while the process became fully automated.



Craftsmanship: Continuous self-reflection to continuously self-improve (iterative testing leading to automation). Problem-Solving/Analytical: Data-driven reduction of planning inaccuracy.

12. IWC reduction by 50% YoY...

12. IWC reduction by 50% YoY...

S: WHT transport operations were suffering from high ad-hoc/cxl rates (ca. 30%), caused by a lack of coordination where S&OP was regularly making intra-week destination changes without visibility to the downstream transport execution impact. 

T: Expose the source of the operational disruption, secure leadership buy-in, and drive a process change to stabilize the plan.

A: I created a new visibility report by accessing IBET databases to measure the magnitude of the Intra-Week Changes (IWC). I then escalated the problem and coordinated a working group to present a formal business case to EU leadership, showcasing a €7Mn entitlement for reducing IWC. 

R: IWC was significantly reduced in the UK (-50% YoY), which reduced the ad-hoc/cxl rate by 300 bps and set a standard for process transparency and coordination.

Trust: Transparency, escalating a cross-functional misalignment issue. Communication/Leadership: Creating visibility and driving consensus on a business-critical change.
